content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

The proposed amendment identifies a real end-to-end bug in `project create-from-bead` alias handling and should be accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-2-2de40109`: Accept
  `project create-from-bead` still rejects valid milestone-aware bead aliases in the exact places cited. `load_bead_detail` only retries `br show --no-db` for refs without dots (`src/cli/project.rs:1674-1684`), and `select_matching_bead_from_show_response` uses raw `bead.id == bead_id` for any dotted query (`src/cli/project.rs:1714-1725`). That means a valid alias like `8.5.3` will not match a returned canonical id like `9ni.8.5.3`, even though `milestone_bead_refs_match` explicitly treats those as equivalent. Separately, `ensure_bead_belongs_to_milestone` still enforces `canonical_bead_id.starts_with("<milestone>.")` (`src/cli/project.rs:1869-1872`), which rejects numeric-milestone alias forms that `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref` intentionally preserves and `milestone_bead_refs_match` accepts (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:3-18,31-42`). The amendment correctly identifies an internally inconsistent lookup/membership path that prevents the new alias support from working end to end.
